varieties.name: Blue
varieties.why: Classic cottage garden herb with celestial blue flowers, cucumber-flavoured leaves for summer drinks and salads, loved by pollinators

varieties.name: Apache
varieties.why: Compact 45cm plant, heavy yields, ideal for containers, RHS AGM

varieties.name: Basket of Fire
varieties.why: Trailing habit, ornamental and edible, bred for hanging containers

varieties.name: Common Chives
varieties.why: Compact clumps, attractive purple flowers, easy perennial for pots

varieties.name: Garlic Chives
varieties.why: Mild garlic flavour, flat leaves, compact growth, white flowers

varieties.name: Confetti
varieties.why: Slow-bolting variety, fine-cut leaves, compact habit for pots

varieties.name: Leisure
varieties.why: Very slow bolting, large leaves, good for succession sowing in pockets

varieties.name: Field Cornflower (Blue)
varieties.why: Traditional cottage garden favourite, bright blue flowers, drought-tolerant, excellent for pollinators and cut flowers

varieties.name: Classic Fantastic
varieties.why: Improved double flowers in shades of blue with attractive frosted white edges, long stems ideal for cutting

varieties.name: Fernleaf
varieties.why: Dwarf 45cm, compact bushy habit bred for containers, slow bolting

varieties.name: Tendergreen
varieties.why: RHS AGM, stringless, reliable cropper in containers

varieties.name: Purple Teepee
varieties.why: Purple pods easy for kids to spot, compact bush, holds above foliage

varieties.name: Safari
varieties.why: Very compact plant, heavy yields of fine filet beans

varieties.name: Patio Mix
varieties.why: Only 30cm tall, no support needed, fragrant blooms, bred for containers

varieties.name: Pink Cupid
varieties.why: Trailing ground-cover type, cascades from pockets, fragrant pink flowers

varieties.name: Mara des Bois
varieties.why: RHS AGM winner, exceptional alpine-strawberry flavour, disease resistant

varieties.name: Flamenco
varieties.why: RHS AGM, everbearing, compact habit ideal for containers

varieties.name: Albion
varieties.why: Heavy cropper, day-neutral, long fruiting season June-October

varieties.name: Dwarf Green Curled
varieties.why: Compact 40cm height, heavy yields, very hardy, RHS recommended

varieties.name: Nero di Toscana
varieties.why: Cavolo nero, upright habit suits pockets, pick outer leaves continuously

varieties.name: Munstead Dwarf
varieties.why: RHS AGM, compact 45cm, excellent drainage in GreenStalk suits it perfectly

varieties.name: Little Gem
varieties.why: RHS AGM, compact cos type, perfect single-pocket size, sweet hearts

varieties.name: Salad Bowl Mixed
varieties.why: Loose-leaf, cut-and-come-again, no heading required, maximises pocket yield

varieties.name: Tom Thumb
varieties.why: Miniature butterhead, tennis-ball size, perfect for single pockets

varieties.name: Boy-O-Boy Mixed
varieties.why: Ultra-dwarf 20cm, prolific blooms, excellent companion plant for pest control

varieties.name: Durango Mixed
varieties.why: Compact 25cm plants, large double blooms, strong pest-deterrent scent

varieties.name: Garden Mint (Spearmint)
varieties.why: Classic flavour, vigorous in pots, GreenStalk contains its spreading roots

varieties.name: Chocolate Mint
varieties.why: After-dinner-mint aroma, compact spreader, fun for kids to smell

varieties.name: Tom Thumb Mixed
varieties.why: Dwarf 25cm mounds, bright edible flowers, ideal compact container variety

varieties.name: Alaska Mixed
varieties.why: Variegated foliage, compact habit, edible leaves and flowers

varieties.name: Greek Oregano
varieties.why: Compact bushy habit, strongest flavour for cooking, drought tolerant

varieties.name: Hot & Spicy
varieties.why: Extra pungent, compact growth, excellent in containers

varieties.name: Moss Curled
varieties.why: Compact bushy growth, decorative, good container variety

varieties.name: Plain Leaved 2 (Flat Leaf)
varieties.why: Stronger flavour than curled, compact rosette, cut-and-come-again

varieties.name: Half Pint
varieties.why: Dwarf variety only 30cm tall, no support needed, bred for containers

varieties.name: Tom Thumb
varieties.why: Heritage dwarf at 25cm, no staking, sweet flavour, RHS recommended

varieties.name: Perpetual Spinach
varieties.why: Cut-and-come-again, bolts far less than true spinach, long harvest

varieties.name: Medania
varieties.why: True spinach, slow to bolt, compact rosette suited to containers

varieties.name: Fiesta Gitana Mixed
varieties.why: Dwarf 25cm, double flowers, compact habit, edible petals

varieties.name: French Breakfast 3
varieties.why: RHS AGM, quick 25-day crop, elongated shape suits shallow pockets

varieties.name: Cherry Belle
varieties.why: Round shape, 22-day maturity, compact roots perfect for shallow containers

varieties.name: Cultivated Rocket
varieties.why: Fast-growing, peppery leaves, cut-and-come-again, compact growth

varieties.name: Wild Rocket
varieties.why: Perennial, stronger peppery taste, slower bolting than cultivated

varieties.name: Common Rosemary
varieties.why: Essential evergreen culinary herb, fragrant aromatic leaves for roasts and potatoes, thrives in containers, low maintenance

varieties.name: Miss Jessop's Upright
varieties.why: RHS AGM, upright columnar habit ideal for containers and tight spaces, excellent flavour, hardy evergreen

varieties.name: Common Sage
varieties.why: Essential culinary herb with soft grey-green leaves, rich flavour for meats and stuffing, attractive perennial sub-shrub

varieties.name: White Lisbon
varieties.why: RHS AGM, fast maturing, slender roots suited to pocket depth

varieties.name: Ishikura
varieties.why: Japanese bunching type, no bulb formation, ideal for tight spacing

varieties.name: Teddy Bear
varieties.why: Dwarf 60cm, fluffy double blooms, no staking, perfect for kids

varieties.name: Little Dorrit
varieties.why: Micro-dwarf 40cm, branching stems, multiple blooms per plant

varieties.name: Greek Dwarf
varieties.why: Compact mounding habit, perfect for small pockets, intense flavour

varieties.name: Minette
varieties.why: Ultra-compact bush variety, 20cm height, bred for pots

varieties.name: Auricula-Eyed Mix
varieties.why: Vigorous heritage variety in salmon, scarlet and purple with well-defined eye pattern, wonderfully fragrant, hardy biennial

varieties.name: Pinocchio
varieties.why: Superb dwarf annual variety at just 15cm, double flowers in attractive colour range, ideal for containers and GreenStalk pockets

varieties.name: Bright Lights
varieties.why: RHS AGM, multi-coloured stems, ornamental and edible, kids love the colours

varieties.name: Lucullus
varieties.why: Compact habit, pale green stems, mild flavour, reliable in pots

varieties.name: Common Thyme
varieties.why: Compact evergreen perennial, thrives in GreenStalk drainage, RHS recommended

varieties.name: Lemon Thyme
varieties.why: Citrus fragrance, low spreading habit, ornamental and culinary

varieties.name: Tumbling Tom Red
varieties.why: Bred for hanging baskets, determinate trailing habit, very productive

varieties.name: Tumbling Tom Yellow
varieties.why: Golden cherry variant, sweet mild flavour, cascading habit

varieties.name: Cherry Falls
varieties.why: Compact cascading habit, heavy trusses, ideal for containers

varieties.name: Common Lemon Balm
varieties.why: Clay-tolerant fragrant herb with intense lemon-mint scent when brushed. Makes excellent herbal tea. Practically indestructible.

varieties.name: Corsican Mint
varieties.why: Thread-thin emerald carpet with powerful peppermint scent when touched. The only walk-on fragrance plant that tolerates clay.

varieties.name: Gran's Favourite
varieties.why: RHS AGM. White with reddish-purple lacing, warm clove fragrance. Compact habit perfect for GreenStalk tiers. Edible flowers.

varieties.name: Attar of Roses
varieties.why: RHS AGM. Deep rose fragrance from foliage — every brush releases extraordinary perfume. Summer display May-October.

varieties.name: Lemon Crispum
varieties.why: Intense lemon scent, variegated leaves, compact habit. Perfect for summer GreenStalk display.

varieties.name: Night-Scented Stock
varieties.why: Dirt-cheap seeds, extraordinary dusk fragrance. Scatter seeds in any tier near seating areas. The best value fragrance plant.

varieties.name: Double-Flowered
varieties.why: Produces flowers for chamomile tea with apple-sweet fragrance. Compact enough for top GreenStalk tiers.
